etymology.description: Depicts a bronze bell in its original form -> lower part evolved into 用, serving as phonetic component -> current form
etymology.interpretation: 甬 (yǒng, 'wine cup') originally depicted a bronze bell, which may suggest an ancient object of cultural significance. Over time, the form of the character shifted, with the lower part evolving into 用 (yòng, 'use'), which was retained as a phonetic component in the character. Despite its current meaning of 'wine cup,' the character maintains this connection to its original pictorial representation.
